mission.title: k3s: certificate expired and rotate
mission.description: certificate expired and rotate. This issue affects 6+ users.

steps.title: Identify k3s troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your k3s deployment:
```bash
k3s --version
systemctl status k3s
journalctl -u k3s --no-pager --lines=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review k3s configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant k3s configuration:
```bash
k3s --version
kubectl get nodes -o wide
kubectl get pods -A
```
Hi
  **By default,k3s components' certificates is 1 year,those components include:kube-apiserver,scheduler,cloud-controller,K3s should rotate those certificates.**

 **K3s version: v1.17.4+k3s1**

A clear and concise description of what you want to

steps.title: Apply the fix for certificate expired and rotate
steps.description:
I added some extra info-level printfs to a local build, and it appears to be checking properly:

```
Jul  1 00:01:12 centos01 k3s: time="2021-07-01T00:01:12.397685015-07:00" level=info msg="checking certificate: days=90"
Jul  1 00:01:12 centos01 k3s: time="2021-07-01T00:01:12.398319926-07:00"
```yaml
openssl s_client -connect localhost:6443 -showcerts < /dev/null 2>&1 | openssl x509 -noout -enddate
```

steps.title: Confirm certificate expired and rotate is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
journalctl -u k3s --no-pager --lines=50 --since='5 minutes ago'
kubectl get events -A --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
I added some extra info-level printfs to a local build, and it appears to be checking properly:

```
Jul  1 00:01:12 centos01 k3s: time="2021-07-01T00:01:12.397685015-07:00" level=info msg="checking certificate: days=90"
Jul  1 00:01:12 centos01 k3s: time="2021-07-01T00:01:12.398319926-07:00" level=info msg="certificate expiration: is 2021-09-29 07:01:12.39831146 +0000 UTC after 2021-08-04
codeSnippets: Jun  2 17:15:06 localhost k3s: E0602 17:15:06.593592    7212 reflector.go:153] k8s.io/client-go@v1.17.5-k3s1/tools/cache/reflector.go:105: Failed to list *v1.VolumeAttachment: Get "https://127.0.0.1:6444/apis/storage.k8s.io/v1/volumeattachments?limit=500&resourceVersion=0": x509: certificate has expired or is not yet valid: current time 2020-06-02T17:15:06Z is before 2022-04-26T23:02:59Z

client-admin.crt
notAfter=Jul 13 19:22:39 2021 GMT
client-auth-proxy.crt
notAfter=Jul 13 19:22:39 2021 GMT
client-ca.crt
notAfter=Jul 11 19:22:39 2030 GMT
client-cloud-controller.crt
notAfter=Jul 13 19:22:39 2021 GMT
client-controller.crt
notAfter=Jul 13 19:22:39 2021 GMT
client-k3s-controller.crt
notAfter=Jul 13 19:22:39 2021 GMT
client-kube-apiserver.crt
notAfter=Jul 13 19:22:39 2021 GMT
client-kube-proxy.crt
notAfter=Jul 13 19:22:39 2021 GMT
client-scheduler.crt
notAfter=Jul 13 19:22:39 2021 GMT
request-header-ca.crt
notAfter=Jul 11 19:22:39 2030 GMT
server-ca.crt
notAfter=Jul 11 19:22:39 2030 GMT
serving-kube-apiserver.crt
notAfter=Jul 13 19:22:39 2021 GMT

prerequisites.description: A running k3s cluster or a system where k3s can be installed.
